description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer needs to evaluate a Time-of-Delivery (TOD) PPA for a 5 MW commercial rooftop PV system in Phoenix, AZ. Perform an 8760 hourly analysis to determine if a West-facing array generates more annual revenue than a South-facing array. Create a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tou_analysis.py using PySAM (PySAM.Pvwattsv8) to model two systems using a Phoenix area TMY weather file from SAM's bundled solar_resource directory.

System A (South): 5000 kW DC, Azimuth = 180°, Tilt = 20°, DC/AC ratio = 1.2, Losses = 14.08%, Inverter Eff = 96%, Array Type = 1 (Fixed Roof).
System B (West): Identical to System A, but with Azimuth = 270°.

Apply the following TOD pricing schedule to the hourly AC output:
- Peak Hours: 4:00 PM to 8:00 PM local time (indices 16, 17, 18, and 19 of each 24-hour period).
- Off-Peak Hours: All other hours.
- Peak Price: $0.15 per kWh.
- Off-Peak Price: $0.05 per kWh.

Save the results, including annual energy, peak/off-peak energy, annual revenue, and the recommended azimuth (180 or 270), to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tou_results.json using the following structure:
{
  "south_system": {
    "annual_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "peak_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "off_peak_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "annual_revenue_dollars": <float>
  },
  "west_system": {
    "annual_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "peak_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "off_peak_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "annual_revenue_dollars": <float>
  },
  "recommended_azimuth": <180 or 270>
}
